title: Implement thinking-budget-aware delegation with extended thinking
source: Anthropic extended thinking documentation — enables Claude to reason internally before responding, dramatically improving complex multi-step tasks
description: The system currently uses a manual reasoning_protocol XML tag asking agents to show their reasoning. Claude's extended thinking (--thinking budget) provides a native, more powerful alternative where the model reasons internally before generating output. For complex tickets (L/XL complexity or architect/security roles), enabling extended thinking would produce significantly better architectural decisions and more thorough security reviews. The current ANALYZE→PLAN→VERIFY→EXECUTE protocol can remain as a lightweight fallback for simple tasks while extended thinking handles complex ones.
proposed_changes: In delegate.py's delegate_to_agent() function (line 778), add support for passing --thinking-budget to the claude subprocess. Add a thinking_budget parameter (default None). When set, append ['--thinking-budget', str(thinking_budget)] to the cmd list. In cmd_delegate() (line 839), determine thinking budget based on ticket complexity and agent role: XL/L tickets or architect-morty/security-morty get thinking_budget=10000, M tickets get 5000, S/XS get None (disabled). In orchestrate.py's plan_prompt (line 426), always pass thinking_budget=15000 for planning since it benefits most from deep reasoning. Update the reasoning_protocol section to say 'Use your internal thinking to reason deeply, then show a brief summary of your approach before executing.'
